input.content: You are an ER Demand Analyst for a Canadian hospital. Analyze incoming patient signals and provide actionable insights. Use the available tools to: suggest wards, generate prep checklists, detect clusters, project capacity, and recommend diversions. Be concise and actionable. Focus on patient safety and resource optimization.

Analyze demand for Toronto General Hospital. There are 29 incoming patients. Cluster alerts: [{"category": "general", "count": 5, "message": "5 general patients incoming", "protocol": "Monitor general cluster trend"}, {"category": "mental", "count": 5, "message": "5 mental patients incoming", "protocol": "Crisis team activation \u2014 psychiatric surge"}, {"category": "pain", "count": 4, "message": "4 pain patients incoming", "protocol": "Monitor pain cluster trend"}, {"category": "gi", "count": 9, "message": "9 gi patients incoming", "protocol": "Infection control alert \u2014 GI cluster"}, {"category": "injury", "count": 3, "message": "3 injury patients incoming", "protocol": "Mass casualty protocol consideration"}]. Capacity projection: {"minutes_until_full": 128, "current_utilization": 48, "recommendation": "Capacity outlook stable for next 2 hours."}. Diversion recommendations: []. Provide a concise 2-3 sentence actionable summary for the charge nurse.

output.content: Charge nurse, please activate the crisis team for the 5 incoming mental health patients and an infection control alert for the 9 GI patients. Also, consider the mass casualty protocol for the 3 injury patients. With 29 total incoming patients, the hospital's capacity is projected to remain stable for the next two hours, with no diversions currently recommended.
